content.problemStatement:
Read problem statements in RussianYou are given a sequence of 16N16N16N positive integers a1,a2,…,a16Na_1,a_2,\ldots,a_{16N}a1​,a2​,…,a16N​. You may shuffle  this sequence in any way you choose, i.e. change it to any one of its permutations. Then, let
x=(a1⊕a2)⊗(a3⊕a4)⊗…⊗(a8N−1⊕a8N) ,x=(a_1 \oplus a_2) \otimes (a_3 \oplus a_4) \otimes \ldots \otimes (a_{8N-1} \oplus a_{8N}) \,,x=(a1​⊕a2​)⊗(a3​⊕a4​)⊗…⊗(a8N−1​⊕a8N​),
y=(a8N+1⊕a8N+2)⊗(a8N+3⊕a8N+4)⊗…⊗(a16N−1⊕a16N) ,y=(a_{8N+1} \oplus a_{8N+2}) \otimes (a_{8N+3} \oplus a_{8N+4}) \otimes \ldots \otimes (a_{16N-1} \oplus a_{16N}) \,,y=(a8N+1​⊕a8N+2​)⊗(a8N+3​⊕a8N+4​)⊗…⊗(a16N−1​⊕a16N​),

where ⊗\otimes⊗ and ⊕\oplus⊕ denote bitwise AND and XOR respectively.

Find the maximum possible value of x−yx-yx−y.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains 16N16N16N space-separated integers a1,a2,…,a16Na_1,a_2,\ldots,a_{16N}a1​,a2​,…,a16N​.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer — the maximum value of x−yx-yx−y.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Since 1⊕1=2⊕2=01 \oplus 1 = 2 \oplus 2 = 01⊕1=2⊕2=0 and 1⊕2=31 \oplus 2 = 31⊕2=3, it is easy to see that we should let a1=a3=a5=a7=2a_1=a_3=a_5=a_7=2a1​=a3​=a5​=a7​=2 and all the remaining elements aia_iai​ should be 111.
